source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

event.top_event: Hand Crush Injury from Pinch Point
actions_taken: ROV Technician/Deckman informed winch operator to lift frame to release hand
Deckman reported to medic on board
Medivac helicopter evacuation to shore for further evaluation
TBRA form to be updated to identify pinch point hazard and red zones
Physical barrier to be designed and installed on launch and recovery system
event.summary: On 04-JUN-2021 at 0930 hours, an ROV Deckman inadvertently placed his left hand in a pinch point between the two cantilever support areas (A-Frame and lower pedestal) of the ROV lifting frame during ROV launch operations on the Seadrill West Neptune. The Deckman immediately contacted the Winch Operator by radio to stop and retract the A-Frame. The Deckman was medivaced to shore for evaluation. Investigation found that the TBRA did not identify the pinch point hazard, the red zone was unidentified, and the Deckman had become complacent during a routine operation.
recommendations: Update TBRA form to identify pinch point hazard between upper A-Frame and lower pedestal
Identify and properly label red zones and safe observation zones for all ROV systems
Design and install physical barrier on launch and recovery system to eliminate pinch point

hazards.name: Pinch Point Between A-Frame and Lower Pedestal
ROV Lifting Frame Extension Over Vessel Side

threats.name: Hand Placement in Unidentified Pinch Point During ROV Launch
Deckman Remaining in Unidentified Red Zone
Complacency Due to Routine Operation Repetition

controls.name: Task Based Risk Assessment Form (TBRA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures prior to task execution
controls.lod_basis: Pre-task hazard identification and risk mitigation planning

supporting_text: the TBRA did not identify the potential pinch point hazard between the upper A-Frame and lower pedestal on the ROV lifting unit
each crew member involved in the task, read the TBRA themselves and signed the document without any open discussion as a team to identify additional hazards

controls.name: Red Zone Identification and Labeling
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from entering hazardous areas during operations
controls.lod_basis: Physical and administrative demarcation of hazardous work areas

supporting_text: The incident location was not identified as a potential red zone during the risk assessment of the task
the Deckman remained in an unidentified Red Zone as the Winch Operator lowered the A-Frame onto the lower pedestal

controls.name: Pre-Dive Inspection and Barrier Installation
controls.barrier_role: Verify equipment condition and safety barriers prior to ROV deployment
controls.lod_basis: Equipment inspection and physical barrier verification

supporting_text: A pre-dive inspection was completed, with barriers in place, and the gates lowered in preparation for the ROV deployment

controls.name: Open Radio Communications During ROV Launch
controls.barrier_role: Enable rapid communication to stop operations in case of emergency

supporting_text: The ROV team always used open radio communications during the launch of the ROV
The Deckman immediately contacted the Winch Operator by radio to stop the operation and retract the A-Frame to remove his hand

controls.name: Deckman Responsibility to Stand Back from Red Zone
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from remaining in hazardous areas during equipment movement

supporting_text: The Deckman's responsibility was to pull the rope to lower the gate on the ROV deck as it is being deployed and stand back out of the Red Zone
After the gate was lowered, the Deckman remained in an unidentified Red Zone as the Winch Operator lowered the A-Frame onto the lower pedestal

controls.name: Physical Barrier on Pinch Point (Planned)
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate pinch point hazard through engineering design
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to remove hazard at source

supporting_text: a physical barrier will be designed and installed on the launch and recovery system to eliminate this pinch point

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
